title: The Seven Virtues Seekers' Journey
description: This coloring page introduces students to the seven classical virtues through symbols and imagery, connecting to the theme of the American Seekers Academy. Students will learn about important character traits while enjoying a creative coloring experience.
instructions: Color each virtue symbol using different colors. Think about what each virtue means as you color. Use lighter colors for backgrounds and darker colors for the main symbols. Try to use colors that you think represent each virtue!

content.image: A large circular emblem with 'American Seekers Academy' at the top in banner style. In the center is a compass rose. Around the compass are seven distinct sections, each containing a classical symbol representing one of the seven virtues: a lion (Courage/Fortitude), scales (Justice), a heart (Love/Charity), an olive branch (Peace/Temperance), a lamp (Wisdom/Prudence), an anchor (Hope/Faith), and wheat sheaf (Humility).

elements.name: Lion
elements.description: Symbol of Courage/Fortitude - the strength to face challenges

elements.name: Scales
elements.description: Symbol of Justice - treating everyone fairly

elements.name: Heart
elements.description: Symbol of Love/Charity - caring for others

elements.name: Olive Branch
elements.description: Symbol of Peace/Temperance - self-control and moderation

elements.name: Lamp
elements.description: Symbol of Wisdom/Prudence - making good choices

elements.name: Anchor
elements.description: Symbol of Hope/Faith - believing in good things to come

elements.name: Wheat Sheaf
elements.description: Symbol of Humility - being modest and kind

learningFacts: The seven virtues were important values in classical education
Ancient Greeks and Romans studied these virtues to become better citizens
These virtues are still important character traits today
The American Seekers Academy encourages students to develop these virtues
Symbols help us remember important ideas and values
